response: TEMPERATURE IS A MEASUREMENT OF THE HEAT ENERGY IN A SUBSTANCE. IT IS A KEY FACTOR IN MANY NATURAL AND MAN-MADE PROCESSES. THE BODY USES TEMPERATURE TO REGULATE ITS FUNCTIONS. WHEN TEMPERATURE RISES, THE BODY MAY SUFFER FROM HEAT STRESS. IN A HOT ENVIRONMENT, THE BODY NEEDS TO COOL DOWN. A COMMON WAY TO DO THIS IS TO USE A COOLING DEVICE OR TO EXERT PHYSICAL EXERTION. THE TEMPERATURE OF THE AIR CAN AFFECT HOW WE FEEL. WHEN TEMPERATURE IS HIGH, WE MAY FEEL THERMALLY UNCOMFORTABLE. A HIGH TEMPERATURE CAN ALSO AFFECT THE PERFORMANCE OF ELECTRONIC EQUIPMENT. IN A HOT ROOM, THE TEMPERATURE CAN BE AFFECTED BY THE SIZE OF THE ROOM AND THE INSULATION. THE TEMPERATURE OF THE WATER IN A BATH CAN BE AFFECTED BY THE TYPE OF HEATER USED. IN A HOT CLIMATE, THE TEMPERATURE IS A KEY FACTOR IN THE SEASONAL CHANGES OF THE CLIMATE.
